title: Half of Luxury Shoppers Prefer Resale, and The RealReal Saw It Coming
text: Nearly half of luxury consumers now consider resale value in purchasing decisions, reflecting a fundamental shift in the luxury market. The RealReal has capitalized on this trend through its supply-unlocking initiatives, retail presence expansion, and enhanced customer experience features. The company's focus on premium consignment through retail stores and brand partnerships has driven higher-value transaction growth. Average order value increased 15% to $646 in Q1 2026, reflecting strong demand for fine jewelry, designer handbags, and luxury timepieces. Management highlighted the flywheel effect as 43% of new consigners originate from the existing buyer base, creating self-reinforcing growth dynamics. The luxury resale market is expanding at nearly 2-3x the rate of first-hand luxury markets, providing significant runway for continued expansion. Consumer preferences are increasingly favoring resale as both an economic and environmental choice, with rising retail prices accelerating adoption. The RealReal's technology infrastructure, including Athena AI for pricing optimization and inventory management, enables efficient platform operations and higher margins. Management projects the luxury resale market will grow from $8.65 billion in 2024 to $13.04 billion by 2030, representing a 7.08% CAGR.
